steps.title: Identify the key pattern in the data
steps.content: Stations near restaurants/convenience stores = subsidized or free charging + few negative reviews. Stations near car rental companies = full-price charging + many negative reviews. Service quality is assumed equal across all sites. The question is: why does price correlate with review negativity?

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
The correct inference must explain why paying full price leads to more negative reviews when service is equal:
  • Choice A — EV owners are usually unaware of the fees for using charging stations.: if users are unaware of fees, that might cause surprise, but the passage says car rental stations require full-price payment — unaware users still pay, and this doesn't explain the restaurant/convenience store pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — EV drivers are more likely to visit areas with many restaurants and convenience stores than they are to visit areas with fewer such businesses.: visit frequency has no bearing on why negative reviews differ between sites with different pricing structures. ✗
  • Choice C — charging stations located near car rental companies are more frequently visited than charging stations located near restaurants and convenience stores.: higher visit frequency might produce more reviews in total, but the study measures the proportion of negative reviews, not the raw count. ✗
  • Choice D — users who are offered a discount have lower expectations of the quality of charging stations than users who are not offered a discount do.: lower expectations explain why subsidized stations (restaurants/convenience stores) get fewer negative reviews even with equivalent service — and why full-price users at car rental stations leave more negative reviews. ✓
That is the correct answer.
